title: Midday journal record (one JSONL line inside journal.jsonl.zst)
description: One record per journaled effect, one JSON object per line (JSONL) inside standard zstd frames — `zstdcat journal.jsonl.zst | grep <kind>` always works. Key order is fixed: tick, tier, kind, cause_id, id[, payload]. `tick` is the sim tick and ids are per-run monotonic counters — record content NEVER contains wall-clock time (D-BUILD-013). Every submission consumes an id, including tier-filtered ones, so FLIGHT bytes are invariant under snapshot/trace enablement and id gaps mark filtered records (D-BUILD-032). C++ counterpart: core/journal/record.h; the journal.record.* selftests pin the byte shape (change together).

tick.description: Sim tick of the effect; non-decreasing over the stream.

tier.description: Record tier: flight = always-on causality skeleton; snapshot = state captures; trace = high-volume diagnostics (spec section 12).

kind.description: Stable dotted identifier of what happened, e.g. "input.key", "bus.trigger", "state.enter".

cause_id.description: The id of the record that caused this one; 0 = external/root. Chains reconstruct causality mechanically.

id.description: Per-run monotonic record id (0 is reserved as the external/root sentinel).

payload.description: Kind-specific structured payload. Omitted entirely when empty.
